commit.message:
Add Daily Challenge feature with streak tracking

Backend:
- get_daily_scene_id(): MD5 hash of UTC date string → deterministic scene for all users
- GET /api/daily: returns scene, date, bonus_multiplier, secs_until_reset
- DB migration: users.streak INTEGER, users.last_daily TEXT
- POST /api/submit: detects daily scene, awards 2× points, updates streak
  (streak increments if last_daily = yesterday, resets to 1 otherwise)
  returns is_daily, daily_bonus, daily_already_done, streak
- GET /api/leaderboard: includes streak per user row
- GET /api/profile: includes streak, daily_done_today, daily_scene_id

Frontend:
- Daily bar above scene grid: scene name + live HH:MM:SS countdown to UTC midnight
- Countdown auto-reloads daily data when it hits 00:00:00
- Gold "DAILY CHALLENGE" badge on the matching scene card
- "★ Daily · 2× pts" pill in recording modal header
- Points panel shows daily bonus amount + 🔥 N-day streak on completion
- Leaderboard: 🔥N streak badge next to username
- My Progress: streak shown in division card

commit.message:
Replace Web Audio waveform with 48-bar CSS-animated gold visualizer

- 48 gold bars (up from 30 red) using var(--gold) colour
- @keyframes wave-dance: flat (3px) → peak height → flat, infinite loop
- Each bar gets randomised CSS custom properties via JS:
    --wh  peak height 20–48px, arched higher at centre
    --wd  duration 0.45–1.1s
    --wdl negative delay -1–0s so bars are staggered at start
- Animation runs only when .waveform-wrap.on is present (during recording)
- Bars are invisible/flat when idle — no layout shift
- startWaveform() no longer needs stream arg; removed Web Audio draw loop

Fix prop_key test: add V2 fallback when V1 API key fails or missing

The test-prop-key endpoint previously required a valid V1 API key.
If the V1 key was invalid or missing, the test always failed even

commit.message:
Two-phase IR loading: destroy old algo before creating new one

Automates the manual workaround (IR→SDN→IR) that always produced
clean audio. Phase 1 destroys the old IRAlgorithm so all JUCE
Convolution background threads are fully stopped. After a silence
fade cycle, Phase 2 creates a brand-new IRAlgorithm and loads the
IR with no residual background activity.

commit.message:
refactor(core): move nextTick/immediate queues into core, replace ops with shared buffers (#32440)

## Summary

Major refactoring of the nextTick/immediate queue infrastructure, moving
it from Node polyfills into deno_core and replacing op-based
communication with shared memory buffers.

### Move nextTick queue into core
- Remove `macrotaskCallback` array and the polling return-value protocol
- Collapse macrotask + nextTick callback arrays into a single
`tickCallback` slot
- Simplify `__drainNextTickAndMacrotasks` to just call the tick callback
+ microtasks (no more while-loop polling)
- Move `FixedQueue`, tick queue, and drain loop
(`processTicksAndRejections`, `runNextTicks`, `queueNextTick`) from
`ext/node/polyfills/_next_tick.ts` into `libs/core/01_core.js`
- `ext/node/polyfills/_next_tick.ts` becomes a thin wrapper:
`nextTick()` handles Node-specific validation, async hooks, and exit
checks, then delegates to `core.queueNextTick()`
- `setTickHook()` allows Node polyfills to inject per-tick async hook
behavior without core depending on Node internals
- `process.ts` no longer registers
`setTickCallback`/`setMacrotaskCallback` — just calls `enableNextTick()`
which sets up the tick hook

### Move immediate queue into core
- Move `setImmediate`/`clearImmediate` queue management from
`ext/node/polyfills/timers.ts` into `libs/core/01_core.js`
- Add async hooks support
(`emitInit`/`emitBefore`/`emitAfter`/`emitDestroy`) for immediates via
`setImmediateHook()`
- Interleave promise rejection processing with nextTick drain loop
- Catch errors in nextTick callbacks and route through
`uncaughtException`

### Replace ops with shared typed array buffers
- Replace `op_has_tick_scheduled` / `op_set_has_tick_scheduled` with a
shared `Uint8Array` (`tickInfo`) backed by `ContextState::tick_info:
Box<[u8; 2]>`
- Replace `op_immediate_count` / `op_immediate_ref_count` /
`op_immediate_set_has_outstanding` / `op_immediate_has_ref_count` with a
shared `Uint32Array` (`immediateInfo`) backed by
`ContextState::immediate_info: Box<[u32; 3]>`
- JS reads/writes the shared buffers directly — no JS-to-Rust boundary
crossing needed
- Shared buffers are passed from Rust to JS via init-only
`__setTickInfo` / `__setImmediateInfo` setter functions during
`store_js_callbacks`
- `__drainNextTickAndMacrotasks` now takes no arguments (reads
`tickInfo` directly)

### Wire up `hasRejectionToWarn`
- Set `tickInfo[1]` in `promise_reject_callback` (Rust) when
`PromiseRejectWithNoHandler`
- `runNextTicks()` now checks `!hasTickScheduled() &&
!hasRejectionToWarn()`, so pending promise rejections are processed even
when no ticks are queued (matching Node.js behavior)
- `processPromiseRejections()` clears the flag after draining

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use NVIDIA Vulkan ICD for Isaac Sim PhysX GPU discovery on ECS

The PhysX "no suitable CUDA GPU found" error was caused by Vulkan
misconfiguration, not CUDA. PhysX discovers GPUs through Vulkan, not
CUDA directly. Previously we only configured Mesa lavapipe (CPU Vulkan),
so PhysX couldn't find the GPU even though PyTorch CUDA worked fine.

Fix: Create nvidia_icd.json manifest pointing to libGLX_nvidia.so.0
(mounted by nvidia-container-toolkit), with lavapipe as fallback.
Isaac Sim replay is now the primary path (export + video); standalone
PyTorch export is kept as fallback only if Isaac Sim fails.

Changes:
- Dockerfile + Dockerfile.unitree: add nvidia_icd.json manifest, remove
  hardcoded lavapipe-only VK_ICD_FILENAMES
- run.sh: VULKAN_SETUP_CMD creates NVIDIA ICD, VK_ICD_FILENAMES prefers
  NVIDIA with lavapipe fallback in all container definitions
- replay.sh: Isaac Sim is primary (export+video), standalone export is
  fallback; Vulkan auto-detection with diagnostics

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Release v2.0.0 — Agent Streaming, Dashboard Modularization, Red Team Overhaul

Major release with architectural changes across the entire system:

Agent Stream Manager: New real-time agent activity streaming with IPC,
JSONL stream files, automatic process reaping, and dashboard integration.
Agents from all stages (Planning, Building, Testing, Red Team) now stream
live output to the Mission Activity Panel.

Dashboard Modularization: Replaced 5,845-line monolithic main.js with
modular ES6 architecture (agent-activity.js, subagent-pool.js, etc).
Added ghost tab reconciliation, periodic state sync, and WebSocket
reconnect handling.

Red Team: New blind_agent_runner.py with parallel agent spawning,
incremental findings to disk (survives timeouts), structured BUG format,
and Artifacts/Red_Team/ workspace output path.

Stage Gate Improvements: Bash write guard now bypasses when no conductor
is running. IPC port passed from dashboard to conductor subprocess.
Silent IPC failures now logged.

Subagent Pool: New pool manager for managing concurrent agent execution
with configurable limits and wave-based scheduling.

Additional: Mission status schema canonicalization, artifact health
monitoring, artifact categorization, exploration graph enhancements,
and numerous bug fixes across af_engine stages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(proxy): redirect server actions on sign-out instead of 401 JSON

Next.js 16's fetchServerAction cannot parse plain JSON 401 responses —
it expects either a valid RSC flight payload or a middleware redirect.
The previous 401 JSON handler caused "An unexpected response was received
from the server" during sign-out when in-flight server actions hit the
proxy without a session.

Consolidated all fetch-based unauthenticated requests (next-action, rsc,
next-router-prefetch, next-router-state-tree) into a single redirect
block. Next.js 16 handles middleware redirects natively for all of these.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(risultante): v2.0 — vectorial three-force dynamics module

Clean, publication-ready module for R(t+1) = R(t) + τ·(1/R + 1 - R):
- Three forces: F_G=1/R (gravity), F_Λ=+1 (coupling), F_D=-R (dissipation)
- Full analysis: forces, potential V(R), trajectories, Lucas bridge, Möbius
- Symplectic structure: det=-1 → char poly → the forces ARE the algebra
- Spectral connection (Piano 11d): disc=5 → Q(√5) → GUE, <r> excess
- Action principle: gradient flow vs discrete D-ND vs linear paths
- CLI + library API: Risultante().full_analysis().report()

Upgrade from Piano 10 exploration (v1) to structured instrument (v2).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix vol annualization for 5s candles — was 3.5x too low

realized_volatility() hardcoded sqrt(525600) assuming 1-min candles.
With 5s candles, per-period returns are sqrt(12)x smaller, making
vol_60m ~0.24 instead of ~0.72 (training scale). This shifted
signed_moneyness distribution, pushing all contracts into extreme
tree leaves (model output collapsed to 3 values: 0.16/0.59/0.90).

Fix: accept candle_seconds parameter, compute periods_per_year
dynamically (365*24*3600/candle_seconds).

commit.message:
CL-33: Generate synthetic contracts with amendments (#10)

* CL-33: Generate synthetic contracts with amendments

Redesign the data generator to produce 5 base contracts + 4 amendments
that override specific clauses (pricing, SLA targets, rent). Each document
encodes metadata in its filename for automatic ingestion detection:
{nn}_{contract_id}_{source_type}_{lang}_v{version}_{effective_date}.pdf

Amendment examples:
- ITSVC-001: rate $250 → $285 → $310 across 3 versions
- LEASE-001: rent 25k → 28.5k PLN, parking added
- SLA-001: P1 response 15min → 5min, credit cap 30% → 50%

Updated README, AGENTS.md, docs/synthetic-data.md, docs/architecture.md,
and notebook 01 to describe amendment support.

commit.message:
feat(pipeline): réutilisation des campagnes passées + fix cluster_snap + surrogate ranker

## Corrections cluster_snap
- Désactivation auto_cluster_snap (causait loss 657 au lieu de 44 en cassant la calibration)
- Filtrage des clusters détectés aux bornes de l'espace de recherche
- Réparation multi-passes (3 passes max) pour résoudre les interactions entre règles
- Clipping des valeurs warm-start avant optimizer.tell() (évite crash distribution)

## Phase 4 — Réutilisation des résultats de campagnes (warm-start + surrogate)
- Nouvelle fonction load_campaign_warm_start() : charge les top-k meilleurs résultats
  des campagnes précédentes depuis DuckDB et les injecte en tête de la warm-start queue
- Activation du SurrogateRanker (NN) pour re-classer toute la queue warm-start
  par loss prédite (seuils progressifs : 500 samples → simple_nn, 2000 → ensemble)
- Script ingest_past_campaigns.py : ingestion one-shot des campagnes existantes
  (génère combined_dataset.csv manquants, parse les logs, insère dans DuckDB)
- Abaissement min_valid de 400 à 10 pour accepter les petites campagnes
- Config YAML : warm_start_campaign_top_k=50, warm_start_campaign_max_loss=500

## Nouveaux modules
- kernel/database/ : CalibrationDatabase (DuckDB) pour stockage centralisé
- kernel/generative/ : cVAE, TabSyn, SurrogateRanker pour génération + ranking
- scripts/ : build_database, train_generative, parallel_campaigns, preflight

## Tests
- 3 nouveaux tests contraintes : cluster_snap hors bornes, interaction multi-règles,
  conflit irréconciliable

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Agents: software-architect, backend-specialist, frontend-specialist, ai-engineer
Common skills: adr, diagram, backlog, acceptance-criteria, user-story, tech-review
Backend skills: endpoint, service, api-review
Frontend skills: component, hook, test, ui-review, accessibility
Agent-AI skills: rule, tool, agent-create
Core rules: architecture/patterns, code/guidelines, po/requirements,

- external_cluster: external models with in-cluster URLs (.svc.cluster.local)
- external_provider: external models with external provider URLs

- Created GetAAModelsFromExternalModels to read from gen-ai-aa-external-models ConfigMap

- Added graceful handling for missing CRDs (returns empty list instead of error)

commit.message:
fix(heroes): eliminate ghost-text artifacts from old-site screenshots

The L5L and Date Zero hero images are full-page screenshots of the old
website (1920x7091 and 1920x9339), not clean photographs. This caused
old navigation, pricing cards, and CTA text to bleed through the
overlay gradients, creating a confusing double-header effect.

L5L / Mastermind / Elite (3 files):
- Shift object-position from top to center 10% — crops out the old
  site navigation that was baked into the top of the image
- Replace 2-layer gradient with 3-layer system: top veil (masks legacy
  text), content gradient (text readability), vignette (edge framing)
- Top gradient: from-black via-black/50 to-transparent
- Bottom gradient strengthened: to-black/20 (was to-black/15)

Date Zero (1 file):
- Strengthen base overlay from bg-black/70 to bg-black/85
- Old pricing cards ($199/$697/$1,497) and "SPOTS ARE LIMITED" text
  now fully masked while image still provides subtle texture

commit.message:
fix: reopen completed tasks with unmerged PRs for merge worker

At startup, the reconciliation loop only promoted `ready` and `unresolved`
tasks with open PRs to `merge_pending`. Tasks marked `complete` whose PRs
were never actually merged (e.g. interrupted merge worker) were invisible
to the merge worker on subsequent runs. Now completed tasks with still-open
GitHub PRs are re-promoted to `merge_pending` during startup reconciliation.

commit.message:
fix: add authorship query expansions so identity queries retrieve creator chunk

Queries like 'who is maintaining this website?' tokenize to words with
no keywords in the creator chunk — MiniSearch keyword retrieval returns
nothing relevant. Add QUERY_EXPANSIONS entries for maintain/author/built/
created/creator/pqctoday/eric amador/softhsmv3 → ['Eric Amador','pqctoday',
'creator','maintainer'] so the creator chunk is always retrieved for
identity-type questions regardless of exact phrasing.

commit.message:
fix: wrap all .toFixed() calls with Number(parseFloat()) in BillingDashboard

Every .toFixed() call in the billing component now uses
Number(parseFloat(value || 0)).toFixed() to prevent crashes when
values are strings, null, or undefined from the DB/API.

commit.message:
refactor: Add Round 4 code review findings (17 todos)

Full codebase review with 11 parallel agents covering PHP,
security, performance, hooks, architecture, JavaScript, code
simplicity, agent-native parity, schema drift, call chains,
and past learnings.

Findings: 1 P1 (polling race), 10 P2, 6 P3.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Activate Location, Product Type, Sales Channels, Internal Notes, and Shopify Category fields

- Location: editable dropdown from managed locations list, saves to jadepuma.location metafield
- Product Type: dropdown from managed product-types list (active only), saves as Shopify productType
- Sales Channels: read-only display of Shopify publications with published/unpublished status pills
- Internal Notes: editable textarea, saves to jadepuma.internal_notes metafield
- Shopify Category: read-only display of product taxonomy category
- GraphQL query extended with resourcePublicationsV2 and productCategory fields
- FormData, isDirty, and handleSave all updated for location and internalNotes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(external_plan): resolve Quick Shot timeout and duplicate plan file (#512) (#514)

* feat(external_plan): add skip_llm parameter to import_external_plan

Allows callers to skip LLM extraction and use fast regex fallback,
preventing timeout when queuing workflows via Quick Shot modal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(queue_workflow): skip LLM and use plan file in-place

- Pass skip_llm=True to avoid timeout from LLM extraction during queue
- Use external plan file path as target_path (no duplicate file)
- Only compute target from plan_path_pattern for pasted plan_content

Fixes #512

* fix(set_workflow_plan): use plan file in-place, no duplicate

Same target_path fix as queue_workflow: when plan_file is provided,
use the file's own path instead of computing from plan_path_pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(ProfileEditModal): simplify unsaved changes detection

Replace 28 lines of manual field comparisons with JSON.stringify.
Key ordering is stable since both values come from profileToFormData().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(ProfileEditModal): add Zod schema for form validation

Adds profileFormSchema for declarative validation, replacing imperative
validateForm. Keeps validateField for real-time blur validation and
explicit API model check for per-agent error display.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(ProfileEditModal): extract useDriverModel hook

Deduplicates driver/model selection logic between BulkApply component
and handleAgentChange. Single source of truth for model-reset-on-
driver-change behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(ProfileEditModal): consolidate validation with Zod schemas

- Use Zod schema directly in validateField instead of manual switch-case
- Add refine() to agentFormSchema requiring model when driver is 'api'
- Normalize agent validation error paths to match field name convention
- Clarify useDriverModel hook docstring scope

Iteration: 1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ProfileEditModal): correct validateField type and remove duplicate validation

- Add ValidatableField type for fields accepting string validation
- Narrow handleBlur field parameter to 'id' | 'repo_root'
- Remove redundant API driver model validation (already in Zod schema)

Iteration: 2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(tests): replace hard-coded date with dynamic resolve_plan_path

Hard-coded "2026-03-04" in pattern_based path assertions would fail on
any other date. Use resolve_plan_path() to compute expected paths
dynamically from the profile's plan_path_pattern.

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
UX: Remplacement de datetime-local par deux inputs date + heure

Meilleure compatibilité cross-browser (Safari, Firefox) pour la
saisie de la date et l'heure d'audience. Le mail généré inclut
désormais l'heure au format JJ/MM/YYYY HH:MM.

commit.message:
fix: Source file viewer — serve real PDF content + reference markers in chat

- /v1/document/get/<doc_id>: Now serves actual PDF binary (generated on-the-fly)
  instead of empty JSON — pdf.js can now render document previews
- Non-PDF docs served as HTML preview pages via window.open
- /v1/document/download/<doc_id>: Proper file download with correct MIME types
- /v1/document/infos: Returns batch document metadata from MOCK_KB_DOCS
- /v1/document/thumbnails: Returns thumbnail data (null fallback to file icons)
- /v1/document/image/<image_id>: New endpoint for chunk embedded images (SVG placeholder)
- SSE answer text now includes ##N$$ reference markers that frontend converts to
  clickable "Fig. N" badges via currentReg regex
- Reference chunks include content field (HTML) for popover display
- Reference chunks include positions data ([page, x1, x2, y1, y2]) for PDF highlighting
- Conversation stores message-level + conversation-level reference arrays
- Added _find_doc(), _generate_mock_pdf(), _get_mock_pdf_for_doc() helpers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
